notes.note: Arpinum was the Volscian hill-town in southern Latium where Cicero (and Marius before him) was born; full Roman citizenship since 188 BC. The municipality elected only a college of three aediles.
Cisalpine Gaul, the province north of the Apennines under the proconsulship of M. Iunius Brutus in this period; Arpinum's revenue-bearing lands lay within it.
Equites Romani: members of the equestrian order, the second of the two senatorial-and-equestrian propertied classes; most provincial and municipal business was carried by men of this status.
At Arpinum the only elected magistracy was a college of three aediles; the year Cicero is writing of, his son Marcus, his nephew Quintus, and his friend M. Caesius held the three slots.
